titleStmt.title: SII 30.15: original edition by N. Marxia Gandhi, A. Padmavathy and S. Swaminathan
No. 15. (A.R. No. 93 of 1915.) Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District Muruganātha temple-on the west wall of the maṇḍapa infront of the same shrine Vīrarājēndra: year 27: 1233 A.D.

summary.p: This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It records that a kaikkōḷa, Kāḷi Avanāsiyāṇ[ḍāṉ] Ellāmvalla Sōḻagaṅgar donated (two) āṉai-achchu sīyakki-paḻañjalāgai for the livelihood of a person who brings fifty pots of water for the sacred bath of the deity Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍināyanār. It seems that the temple officials who received the amount, allotted a plot of land and agreed to remit paddy for the above purpose.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
kovirācakecaripanmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī virarācentira tevaṟkut tiruveḻuttiṭ
ṭuc cellāniṉṟa tirunelli yāṇṭu Irupatteḻāvatu cittirai mātamutal vaṭaparicāra nāṭṭil cevūrili
rukkum kaikko kāḷi Avanāciyāṇ Ellāmavalla coḻakkaṅkar Āḷuṭaiyā
r tirumurukanpūṇṭi nāyanāṟku nāḷ Onṟukku AiñpatiṉRead- aimpatiṉkuṭam tirumañcaṇam Eṭup
pānukkuc civaṉattukku Oṭukkiṉa Āṉaiaccuc ciyakkip paḻañcalākai raṇṭu tara
ttukku Oṭukkiṉa Accuperaṭṭi nāṭṭi paḻaṅkaṟāyanil Ūrum Ūrāḷika ḷomum Āḷuṭaiyār tirumuruka
npūṇṭi nāyanār cipaṇṭāratte rām kaṇakkaiyāli yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava Āṉaiaccuc ciriyak
ki paḻañcalākai raṇṭum koṇṭom koṇṭapaṭiyāvatu ve kkup palicaiyā
vatu Āṇṭu Ac vacam nelk kaṇapponnāṭu nāḻiyāl panni kkum Aḷantu va
ruvomākavum ṟṟi cani nāṭṭu vaḻi� lukku meṟku
ṟṟukkut teṟku patinañcā yan nāṟpattiyiraṇṭi ma caloka
ku meṟku tti ṭiru kuṭittāṟṟukkut teṟku
koṇṭa tā kkacale Innānkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nilamum
me ma coḻakkuṭittāṟṟukku vaṭakkum Avināciyāṇṭār kuṭittāṟṟukku teṟkum
Inṉāṅkellaiyuṭpaṭṭa nilam Ettāṟiraṇṭum paṇa
rukalakka yum cantirātittavaraiyum Aḷantu varuvomākavum Itu cempiluñ cilaiyilum veṭṭik koḷvatā
Ūrum Ūrāḷikaḷom Innāyanā
Digital edition of SII 30.15 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
